text: 0.3g of fat. cholesterol adds to the tradeoff. for meats, fat quality matters a lot once the protein is already there.
0.2g of carbs. everything else is fairly underwhelming. in this category, carbs barely matter.
24g of protein. bioavailability and amino acid quality are both strong. strong protein is expected here, so the rest of the profile decides how high it climbs.
vitamin b12 at 54% daily value stands out most. that gives this food real vitamin support.
zinc at 10% daily value stands out most. useful, but still pretty limited.

pros first: easy to centre a meal around. practical meal value matters. complete protein delivery is reliable. the category is supposed to do this well. gives a straightforward protein solution. the category often wins on simplicity and reliability.

the drawbacks next: cholesterol headline is hard to ignore. even if the full story is more nuanced, the category score should reflect how extreme the number looks. quality swings a lot by cut and processing. the category can look very different depending on what version you actually buy. cost and storage can be a pain. practical downsides still count.
B tier. Best when you want efficient protein and can accept the category tradeoffs that come with it.
